verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo obscuratum est aurum,  mutatus est color optimus !  dispersi sunt lapides sanctuarii  in capite omnium platearum !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵיכָה֙ יוּעַ֣ם זָהָ֔ב יִשְׁנֶ֖א הַכֶּ֣תֶם הַטּ֑וֹב תִּשְׁתַּפֵּ֙כְנָה֙ אַבְנֵי־קֹ֔דֶשׁ בְּרֹ֖אשׁ כׇּל־חוּצֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Aleph. How is the gold become dim, the finest colour is changed, the stones of the sanctuary are scattered in the top of every street?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka on kulta niin mustunut, ja jalo kulta niin muuttunut? pyhät kivet ovat joka kadun päässä hajoitetut.
verses.king_james: How is the gold become dim! how is the most fine gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary are poured out in the top of every street.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii Sion inclyti,  et amicti auro primo :  quomodo reputati sunt in vasa testea,  opus manuum figuli !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנֵ֤י צִיּוֹן֙ הַיְקָרִ֔ים הַמְסֻלָּאִ֖ים בַּפָּ֑ז אֵיכָ֤ה נֶחְשְׁבוּ֙ לְנִבְלֵי־חֶ֔רֶשׂ מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יְדֵ֥י יוֹצֵֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Beth. The noble sons of Sion, and they that were clothed with the best gold: how are they esteemed as earthen vessels, the work of the potter's hands?
verses.finnish_biblia: Zionin kempit pojat, puhtaan kullan verraksi luetut, kuinka ne ovat savi-astian kaltaiseksi arvatut, jotka savenvalaja tekee?
verses.king_james: The precious sons of Zion, comparable to fine gold, how are they esteemed as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et lamiæ nudaverunt mammam,  lactaverunt catulos suos :  filia populi mei crudelis  quasi struthio in deserto.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־[תַּנִּים֙] (תנין) חָ֣לְצוּ שַׁ֔ד הֵינִ֖יקוּ גּוּרֵיהֶ֑ן בַּת־עַמִּ֣י לְאַכְזָ֔ר (כי ענים) [כַּיְעֵינִ֖ים] בַּמִּדְבָּֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ghimel. Even the sea monsters have drawn out the breast, they have given suck to their young: the daughter of my people is cruel, like the ostrich in the desert.
verses.finnish_biblia: Lohikärmeet taritsevat nisiä pojillensa ja imettävät heitä; mutta minun kansani tyttären täytyy armotoinna olla, niinkuin yökkö korvessa.
verses.king_james: Even the sea monsters draw out the breast, they give suck to their young ones: the daughter of my people is become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhæsit lingua lactentis  ad palatum ejus in siti ;  parvuli petierunt panem,  et non erat qui frangeret eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דָּבַ֨ק לְשׁ֥וֹן יוֹנֵ֛ק אֶל־חִכּ֖וֹ בַּצָּמָ֑א עֽוֹלָלִים֙ שָׁ֣אֲלוּ לֶ֔חֶם פֹּרֵ֖שׂ אֵ֥ין לָהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Daleth. The tongue of the sucking child hath stuck to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the little ones have asked for bread, and there was none to break it unto them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Imeväisten kieli tarttuu suun lakeen janon tähden; nuoret lapsukaiset anovat leipää, ja ei ole ketään, joka heille sitä jakaa.
verses.king_james: The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread, and no man breaketh it unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui vescebantur voluptuose,  interierunt in viis ;  qui nutriebantur in croceis,  amplexati sunt stercora.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָאֹֽכְלִים֙ לְמַ֣עֲדַנִּ֔ים נָשַׁ֖מּוּ בַּחוּצ֑וֹת הָאֱמֻנִים֙ עֲלֵ֣י תוֹלָ֔ע חִבְּק֖וּ אַשְׁפַּתּֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: He. They that were fed delicately have died in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet have embraced the dung.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jotka ennen söivät herkullisesti, ne ovat kadulla nääntyneet; jotka ennen olivat silkillä vaatetetut, niiden täytyy nyt loassa maata.
verses.king_james: They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et major effecta est iniquitas filiæ populi mei  peccato Sodomorum,  quæ subversa est in momento,  et non ceperunt in ea manus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגְדַּל֙ עֲוֺ֣ן בַּת־עַמִּ֔י מֵֽחַטַּ֖את סְדֹ֑ם הַֽהֲפוּכָ֣ה כְמוֹ־רָ֔גַע וְלֹא־חָ֥לוּ בָ֖הּ יָדָֽיִם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Vau. And the iniquity of the daughter of my people is made greater than the sin of Sodom, which was overthrown in a moment, and hands took nothing in her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun kansani tyttären synti on suurempi kuin Sodoman synti, joka äkisti kukistettiin ja ei yksikään käsi siihen ruvennut.
verses.king_james: For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom, that was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands stayed on her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Candidiores Nazaræi ejus nive,  nitidiores lacte,  rubicundiores ebore antiquo,  sapphiro pulchriores.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זַכּ֤וּ נְזִירֶ֙יהָ֙ מִשֶּׁ֔לֶג צַח֖וּ מֵחָלָ֑ב אָ֤דְמוּ עֶ֙צֶם֙ מִפְּנִינִ֔ים סַפִּ֖יר גִּזְרָתָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Zain. Her Nazarites were whiter than snow, purer than milk, more ruddy than the old ivory, fairer than the sapphire.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen nasirinsa olivat puhtaammat kuin lumi ja valkeammat kuin rieska; heidän ihonsa oli punaisempi kuin koralli, heidän kauneutensa niinkuin saphir.
verses.king_james: Her Nazarites were purer than snow, they were whiter than milk, they were more ruddy in body than rubies, their polishing was of sapphire:

verses.latin_vulgate: Denigrata est super carbones facies eorum  et non sunt cogniti in plateis ;  adhæsit cutis eorum ossibus :  aruit, et facta est quasi lignum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָשַׁ֤ךְ מִשְּׁחוֹר֙ תׇּֽאֳרָ֔ם לֹ֥א נִכְּר֖וּ בַּחוּצ֑וֹת צָפַ֤ד עוֹרָם֙ עַל־עַצְמָ֔ם יָבֵ֖שׁ הָיָ֥ה כָעֵֽץ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Heth. Their face is now made blacker than coals, and they are not known in the streets: their skin hath stuck to their bones, it is withered, and is become like wood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt on heidän muotonsa niin mustaksi muuttunut, ettei heitä kaduilla tuta taideta; heidän nahkansa riippuu heidän luissansa, se kuivettui niinkuin puu.
verses.king_james: Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered, it is become like a stick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius fuit occisis gladio  quam interfectis fame,  quoniam isti extabuerunt consumpti  a sterilitate terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טוֹבִ֤ים הָיוּ֙ חַלְלֵי־חֶ֔רֶב מֵֽחַלְלֵ֖י רָעָ֑ב שֶׁ֣הֵ֤ם יָזֻ֙בוּ֙ מְדֻקָּרִ֔ים מִתְּנוּבֹ֖ת שָׂדָֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Teth. It was better with them that were slain by the sword, than with them that died with hunger: for these pined away being consumed for want of the fruits of the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miekalla tapetuille oli parempi kuin niille, jotka nälkään kuolivat, jotka nääntymän ja hukkuman piti maan hedelmän puuttumisesta.
verses.king_james: They that be slain with the sword are better than they that be slain with hunger: for these pine away, stricken through for want of the fruits of the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manus mulierum misericordium  coxerunt filios suos ;  facti sunt cibus earum  in contritione filiæ populi mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְדֵ֗י נָשִׁים֙ רַחֲמָ֣נִיּ֔וֹת בִּשְּׁל֖וּ יַלְדֵיהֶ֑ן הָי֤וּ לְבָרוֹת֙ לָ֔מוֹ בְּשֶׁ֖בֶר בַּת־עַמִּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Jod. The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laupiaammat vaimot ovat omia lapsiansa keittäneet ruaksensa, minun kansani tyttären surkeudessa.
verses.king_james: The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Complevit Dominus furorem suum,  effudit iram indignationis suæ :  et succendit ignem in Sion,  et devoravit fundamenta ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּלָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־חֲמָת֔וֹ שָׁפַ֖ךְ חֲר֣וֹן אַפּ֑וֹ וַיַּצֶּת־אֵ֣שׁ בְּצִיּ֔וֹן וַתֹּ֖אכַל יְסֹדֹתֶֽיהָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Caph. The Lord hath accomplished his wrath, he hath poured out his fierce anger: and he hath kindled a fire in Sion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra on vihansa täyttänyt, hän on vuodattanut julman vihansa, ja Zionissa tulen sytyttänyt, joka myös hänen perustuksensa polttanut on.
verses.king_james: The Lord hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger, and hath kindled a fire in Zion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non crediderunt reges terræ,  et universi habitatores orbis,  quoniam ingrederetur hostis et inimicus  per portas Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֤א הֶאֱמִ֙ינוּ֙ מַלְכֵי־אֶ֔רֶץ (וכל) [כֹּ֖ל] יֹשְׁבֵ֣י תֵבֵ֑ל כִּ֤י יָבֹא֙ צַ֣ר וְאוֹיֵ֔ב בְּשַׁעֲרֵ֖י יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Lamed. The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world would not have believed, that the adversary and the enemy should enter in by the gates of Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei kuninkaat maan päällä sitä olisi uskoneet, eikä kaikki maan piirin asuvaiset, että vihamiehen ja vihollisen piti tuleman Jerusalemin portista sisälle.
verses.king_james: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world, would not have believed that the adversary and the enemy should have entered into the gates of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter peccata prophetarum ejus,  et iniquitates sacerdotum ejus,  qui effuderunt in medio ejus  sanguinem justorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵֽחַטֹּ֣אות נְבִיאֶ֔יהָ עֲוֺנֹ֖ת כֹּהֲנֶ֑יהָ הַשֹּׁפְכִ֥ים בְּקִרְבָּ֖הּ דַּ֥ם צַדִּיקִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Mem. For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta se on hänen prophetainsa syntein tähden ja hänen pappeinsa pahain tekoin tähden, jotka siinä vanhurskasten veren vuodattivat.
verses.king_james: For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her,

verses.latin_vulgate: Erraverunt cæci in plateis,  polluti sunt in sanguine ;  cumque non possent,  tenuerunt lacinias suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָע֤וּ עִוְרִים֙ בַּֽחוּצ֔וֹת נְגֹֽאֲל֖וּ בַּדָּ֑ם בְּלֹ֣א יֽוּכְל֔וּ יִגְּע֖וּ בִּלְבֻשֵׁיהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Nun. They have wandered as blind men in the streets, they were defiled with blood: and when they could not help walking in it, they held up their skirts.
verses.finnish_biblia: He kävivat sinne ja tänne kaduilla, niinkuin sokiat, ja olivat itsensä vereen saastuttaneet, ja ei taitaneet heidän vaatteisiinsa ruveta.
verses.king_james: They have wandered as blind men in the streets, they have polluted themselves with blood, so that men could not touch their garments.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recedite polluti, clamaverunt eis ;  recedite, abite, nolite tangere :  jurgati quippe sunt, et commoti dixerunt inter gentes :  Non addet ultra ut habitet in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ס֣וּרוּ טָמֵ֞א קָ֣רְאוּ לָ֗מוֹ ס֤וּרוּ ס֙וּרוּ֙ אַל־תִּגָּ֔עוּ כִּ֥י נָצ֖וּ גַּם־נָ֑עוּ אָֽמְרוּ֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖פוּ לָגֽוּר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Samech. Depart you that are defiled, they cried out to them: Depart, get ye hence, touch not: for they quarrelled, and being removed, they said among the Gentiles: He will no more dwell among them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan he huusivat heitä: välttäkäät, saastaiset, välttäkäät, älkäät mihinkään ruvetko; sillä he välttivät ja pakenivat, niin että myös pakanain seassa sanottiin: ei he kauvan siellä pysy.
verses.king_james: They cried unto them, Depart ye; it is unclean; depart, depart, touch not: when they fled away and wandered, they said among the heathen, They shall no more sojourn there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facies Domini divisit eos,  non addet ut respiciat eos ;  facies sacerdotum non erubuerunt,  neque senum miserti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּנֵ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ חִלְּקָ֔ם לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖יף לְהַבִּיטָ֑ם פְּנֵ֤י כֹהֲנִים֙ לֹ֣א נָשָׂ֔אוּ (זקנים) [וּזְקֵנִ֖ים] לֹ֥א חָנָֽנוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Phe. The face of the Lord hath divided them, he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests, neither had they pity on the ancient.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden on Herran viha heitä hajoittanut, ja ei enään katso heidän päällensä, ettei he pappeja kunnioittaneet eikä vanhoja armahtaneet.
verses.king_james: The anger of the Lord hath divided them; he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests, they favoured not the elders.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum adhuc subsisteremus, defecerunt oculi nostri  ad auxilium nostrum vanum ;  cum respiceremus attenti ad gentem  quæ salvare non poterat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (עודינה) [עוֹדֵ֙ינוּ֙] תִּכְלֶ֣ינָה עֵינֵ֔ינוּ אֶל־עֶזְרָתֵ֖נוּ הָ֑בֶל בְּצִפִּיָּתֵ֣נוּ צִפִּ֔ינוּ אֶל־גּ֖וֹי לֹ֥א יוֹשִֽׁעַ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ain. While we were yet standing, our eyes failed, expecting help for us in vain, when we looked attentively towards a nation that was not able to save.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuitenkin meidän silmämme kurkistelevat tyhjäin avun jälkeen, siihenasti että he väsyivät, kuin me odotimme sitä kansaa, joka ei meitä auttaa voinut.
verses.king_james: As for us, our eyes as yet failed for our vain help: in our watching we have watched for a nation that could not save us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lubricaverunt vestigia nostra  in itinere platearum nostrarum ;  appropinquavit finis noster, completi sunt dies nostri,  quia venit finis noster.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צָד֣וּ צְעָדֵ֔ינוּ מִלֶּ֖כֶת בִּרְחֹבֹתֵ֑ינוּ קָרַ֥ב קִצֵּ֛נוּ מָלְא֥וּ יָמֵ֖ינוּ כִּי־בָ֥א קִצֵּֽנוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Sade. Our steps have slipped in the way of our streets, our end draweth near: our days are fulfilled, for our end is come.
verses.finnish_biblia: He väijyvät meidän askeleitamme, niin ettemme rohkene meidän kaduillamme käydä; meidän loppumme on lähestynyt, meidän päivämme ovat täytetyt, sillä meidän loppumme on tullut.
verses.king_james: They hunt our steps, that we cannot go in our streets: our end is near, our days are fulfilled; for our end is come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Velociores fuerunt persecutores nostri  aquilis cæli ;  super montes persecuti sunt nos,  in deserto insidiati sunt nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַלִּ֤ים הָיוּ֙ רֹדְפֵ֔ינוּ מִנִּשְׁרֵ֖י שָׁמָ֑יִם עַל־הֶהָרִ֣ים דְּלָקֻ֔נוּ בַּמִּדְבָּ֖ר אָ֥רְבוּ לָֽנוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Coph. Our persecutors were swifter than the eagles of the air: they pursued us upon the mountains, they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Meidän vainoojamme olivat nopiammat kuin kotka taivaan alla; vuorilla he myös meitä vainosivat, ja korvessa he meitä vartioitsivat.
verses.king_james: Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus oris nostri, christus Dominus,  captus est in peccatis nostris,  cui diximus : In umbra tua  vivemus in gentibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ר֤וּחַ אַפֵּ֙ינוּ֙ מְשִׁ֣יחַ יְהֹוָ֔ה נִלְכַּ֖ד בִּשְׁחִיתוֹתָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַ֔רְנוּ בְּצִלּ֖וֹ נִֽחְיֶ֥ה בַגּוֹיִֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Res. The breath of our mouth, Christ the Lord, is taken in our sins: to whom we said: Under thy shadow we shall live among the Gentiles.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herran voideltu, joka meidän turvamme oli, on vangittu heidän verkkoihinsa, josta me sanoimme, että me hänen varjonsa alla elämme pakanain seassa.
verses.king_james: The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the Lord, was taken in their pits, of whom we said, Under his shadow we shall live among the heathen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaude et lætare, filia Edom,  quæ habitas in terra Hus !  ad te quoque perveniet calix : inebriaberis,  atque nudaberis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׂ֤ישִׂי וְשִׂמְחִי֙ בַּת־אֱד֔וֹם (יושבתי) [יוֹשֶׁ֖בֶת] בְּאֶ֣רֶץ ע֑וּץ גַּם־עָלַ֙יִךְ֙ תַּעֲבׇר־כּ֔וֹס תִּשְׁכְּרִ֖י וְתִתְעָרִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Sin. Rejoice, and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Hus: to thee also shall the cup come, thou shalt be made drunk, and naked.
verses.finnish_biblia: Iloitse ja riemuitse, sinä Edomin tytär, joka asut Utsin maalla; sillä sen maljan pitää myös sinulle tuleman, ja sinun pitää myös juopuman, ja alasti itses riisuman.
verses.king_james: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Uz; the cup also shall pass through unto thee: thou shalt be drunken, and shalt make thyself naked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Completa est iniquitas tua, filia Sion :  non addet ultra ut transmigret te.  Visitavit iniquitatem tuam, filia Edom ;  discooperuit peccata tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּם־עֲוֺנֵךְ֙ בַּת־צִיּ֔וֹן לֹ֥א יוֹסִ֖יף לְהַגְלוֹתֵ֑ךְ פָּקַ֤ד עֲוֺנֵךְ֙ בַּת־אֱד֔וֹם גִּלָּ֖ה עַל־חַטֹּאתָֽיִךְ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Thau. Thy iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Sion, he will no more carry thee away into captivity: he hath visited thy iniquity, O daughter of Edom, he hath discovered thy sins.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sinun vääryydelläs on loppu, sinä Zionin tytär, ei hän enään anna sinua viedä pois, mutta sinun vääryytes, Edomin tytär, hän etsii, ja sinun syntis ilmoittaa.
verses.king_james: The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Zion; he will no more carry thee away into captivity: he will visit thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom; he will discover thy sins.
